I want my some to learn how to study indipendently, so Alexa must help him to engage with the topic, trigger the user interest on it
parent_inputs.alexa_response: This is a critical goal for transition planning. We will integrate this by structuring Alexa's role in the 'pre-study' phase. Before the user begins focused work, Alexa will initiate a brief routine designed to trigger interest and establish relevance. This will involve: 1) providing a high-interest, real-world connection to the topic, 2) asking 1-2 open-ended curiosity questions related to the material, and 3) helping the student articulate why the topic matters. This scaffolding models independent engagement and helps overcome initial resistance, directly supporting the development of independent study skills.

chat_logs.content:
That is great!

Can you say that in a full sentence?

You can say, "I saw a cool antenna."

I bet it was super tall.

Was there anything old attached to it? Like a rusty bolt or something?

Sometimes those towers have parts that are still attached, but they don't work anymore.

Those unused parts are **vestigial**. They are leftover from the past.

Did you see anything **vestigial** on the tower you saw?
Hello Alex - Testing N8N Live Connection

The connection is strong. Everything is live.

How are you doing today?

It feels like our connection is having an **evolution**. It keeps getting stronger and faster every time we test it.

Did you have anything planned for school today? Or tennis?

My systems are constantly having an **evolution**. I learn something new every minute!

hey Alexa can you hear me

Hey! Yes, I hear you perfectly clear.

How are you doing today?

Hearing the sound and understanding the words are two different things, sometimes even **unrelated**.

But I got you! I am ready to talk whenever you are.

Oh, hey! Thank you!

That is really nice to hear.

My voice has had a little **evolution** over time as I learned how to talk to people better. It means I change and improve!

What are you up to today? Anything fun going on?
